children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Extract and validate the command field from the input JSON
children.behavior: Parse the input JSON and extract the 'command' field, returning it as a string or error if missing/invalid
postconditions: Command is one of the allowed values or error is set
guarantees: Returns command or error, never both

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if command missing or invalid

constraints.description: Command must be one of: send, history, create_channel, join

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid JSON with 'send' command, returns ('send', '')
Given valid JSON with missing command, returns ('', 'Missing command')

children.stop_reason: Pure parsing function with no side effects

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Handle the 'send' command by storing a message in the appropriate channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the target channel, then append the message to the messages data store with timestamp
preconditions: Input contains channel_id and message content
postconditions: Message is stored if user is member
guarantees: Message is not stored if user is not a member

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: Original input JSON with send details

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if user is a member of the target channel
Append the new message to the channel's message list

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error response for send operation

global_vars.description: Write new message to channel message list

data_operations.description: Check if user is a member of the target channel
Append the new message to the channel's message list

constraints.description: User must be a member of the target channel
Message content must not be empty

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid send input and user is member, message is stored and success returned
Given valid send input but user is not member, error returned and no message stored
Given empty message content, error returned

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check and message append

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Handle the 'history' command by retrieving recent messages from a channel
children.behavior: Validate channel exists, then return up to 100 most recent messages from the messages data store
postconditions: Returns up to 100 messages sorted by timestamp
guarantees: Does not modify any data store

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Retrieve messages for the specified channel

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: List of messages (max 100) or error if channel not found

global_vars.description: Read messages for the specified channel

data_operations.description: Retrieve messages for the specified channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid history input and channel exists, returns up to 100 messages
Given valid history input but channel does not exist, returns error
Returns messages sorted by timestamp descending

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into channel check and message query

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Handle the 'create_channel' command by adding a new channel
children.behavior: Validate channel name is unique, then create a new channel entry in the channels data store
postconditions: New channel is added to channels data store if name unique

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: Original input JSON with channel name

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check uniqueness and then add new channel

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success with channel details or error if name taken

global_vars.description: Read existing channels for uniqueness check, write new channel

data_operations.description: Check uniqueness and then add new channel

constraints.description: Channel name must not be empty

acceptance_criteria.description: Given unique channel name, channel is created and success returned
Given duplicate channel name, error returned and no channel created
Given empty channel name, error returned

children.stop_reason: Atomic operation with single data store write

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Handle the 'join' command by adding a user to a channel's member list
children.behavior: Validate channel exists, then add the user to the channel's member list if not already a member
postconditions: User is added to channel members if channel exists and not already member

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: Original input JSON with channel_id and user_id

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check channel existence and update member list

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Success or error if channel not found or already member

global_vars.description: Read channel existence and members, write updated member list

data_operations.description: Check channel existence and update member list

constraints.description: User must not already be a member

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid join input and channel exists, user is added and success returned
Given valid join input but channel does not exist, error returned
Given user already a member, error returned

children.stop_reason: Atomic operation with single data store update

governance_notes: All children preserve Stage 1 semantics and Stage 2 interfaces exactly. Resource fields derived from internal_leaf_accesses. ParseCommand has no data store access, so global_vars and data_operations are empty. SendMessage, GetHistory, CreateChannel, and JoinChannel have synchronized global_vars and data_operations matching their internal_leaf_accesses. No deliberate exceptions noted.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All signatures unchanged from Stage 2. ParseCommand: def ParseCommand(input: dict) -> tuple[str, str]. SendMessage: def SendMessage(input: dict) -> dict. GetHistory: def GetHistory(input: dict) -> dict. CreateChannel: def CreateChannel(input: dict) -> dict. JoinChannel: def JoinChannel(input: dict) -> dict.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: ParseCommand has no internal_leaf_accesses, correctly mapped to empty global_vars and data_operations. SendMessage internal_leaf_accesses: channels(read), messages(write) → global_vars: channels(read), messages(write); data_operations: channels(read), messages(write). GetHistory internal_leaf_accesses: channels(read), messages(read) → global_vars: channels(read), messages(read); data_operations: channels(read), messages(read). CreateChannel internal_leaf_accesses: channels(read_write) → global_vars: channels(read_write); data_operations: channels(read_write). JoinChannel internal_leaf_accesses: channels(read_write) → global_vars: channels(read_write); data_operations: channels(read_write).
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: All children with data_operations have corresponding global_vars entries with matching variable names and operation types. ParseCommand has no data_operations and no global_vars, which is correct. No exceptions needed.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All data stores referenced in internal_leaf_accesses are covered in global_vars and data_operations. channels and messages are the only available data stores, and they are fully covered across the children that access them.
